title: Rachel Aviv’s Act of Revision: On You Won’t Get Free of It
description: “I had written three stories with three lost babies, all of which I had left out.”
hook: Rachel Aviv rewrote her story three times before realizing she'd deleted the same loss each time. What we leave out of our own narratives.

title: “We’re Magnifying Stupid”: Jeff Tremaine on Jackass: Best and Last
description: In Jackass: Best and Last (2026), some of the cast get unexpectedly wistful about the possibility that this will be the final chapter of the brilliantly sophomoric series. The man who has been behind the camera and orchestrating the madness from the start, director Jeff Tremaine, has been feeling a bit emotional about it, too. “I rea
hook: The Jackass guys got unexpectedly philosophical about mortality and stupidity as art. Jeff Tremaine on why it might actually end.

title: On Description
description: "If a depiction only distinguishes one thing from another, what else is lost?"
hook: When every description becomes a choice about what to erase, what survives? A meditation on the ethics of detail.

title: 'The Perfect Moment' makes the case that culture wars have 'completely eaten America'
description: Author Isaac Butler explains how the religious right embraced culture wars, starting in the 1970s with school book bans, and continuing now with the Trump administration's efforts to defund the NEA.
hook: How the religious right weaponized culture wars starting in the 1970s—and why Trump's defunding of the NEA is actually the endgame.

title: Philip Guston’s Lines of Poetry
description: A show of his works from 1964–1978 focuses on the artist’s transformation and on his wife, poet Musa McKim, as a principal supporter and source of inspiration.
hook: Philip Guston's wife Musa McKim wasn't just his muse—she was the architect of his artistic transformation. A story told through the work itself.
